sections.label: Section 1397
sections.original: σὸν ἔργον ὦ καινῶν ἐπῶν κινητὰ καὶ μοχλευτὰ
sections.translation: It is thy business , thou author and upheaver of new words , to seek some means of persuasion , so that you shall seem to speak justly .

sections.label: Section 1399
sections.original: ὡς ἡδὺ καινοῖς πράγμασιν καὶ δεξιοῖς ὁμιλεῖν ,
sections.translation: How pleasant it is to be acquainted with new and clever things ,

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: καὶ τῶν καθεστώτων νόμων ὑπερφρονεῖν δύνασθαι .
sections.translation: and to be able to despise the established laws ! For I , when I applied my mind to horsemanship alone , used not to be able to utter three words before I made a mistake ; but now , since he himself has made me cease from these pursuits , and I am acquainted with subtle thoughts , and arguments , and speculations ,

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: οἶμαι διδάξειν ὡς δίκαιον τὸν πατέρα κολάζειν .
sections.translation: I think I shall demonstrate that it is just to chastise one ’ s father .

sections.label: Section 1406
sections.original: ἵππευε τοίνυν νὴ Δίʼ , ὡς ἔμοιγε κρεῖττόν ἐστιν
sections.translation: Ride , then , by Jupiter ! Since it is better for me to keep a team of four horses than to be killed with a beating .

sections.label: Section 1408
sections.original: ἐκεῖσε δʼ ὅθεν ἀπέσχισάς με τοῦ λόγου μέτειμι ,
sections.translation: I will pass over to that part of my discourse where you interrupted me ; and first I will ask you this : Did you beat me when I was a boy ?

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: ἔγωγέ σʼ εὐνοῶν τε καὶ κηδόμενος .
sections.translation: I did , through good-will and concern for you .

sections.label: Section 1410b
sections.original: εἰπὲ δή μοι ,
sections.translation: Pray tell me , is it not just that I also should be well inclined toward you in the same way , and beat you , since this is to be well inclined-to give a beating ? For why ought your body to be exempt from blows and mine not ? And yet I too was born free .

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: κλάουσι παῖδες , πατέρα δʼ οὐ κλάειν δοκεῖς ; ⋯
sections.translation: The boys weep , and do you not think it is right that a father should weep ? You will say that it is ordained by law that this should be the lot of boys . But I would reply , that old men are boys twice over , and that it is the more reasonable that the old should weep than the young , inasmuch as it is less just that they should err .

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδαμοῦ νομίζεται τὸν πατέρα τοῦτο πάσχειν .
sections.translation: It is nowhere ordained by law that a father should suffer this .

sections.label: Section 1421
sections.original: οὔκουν ἀνὴρ ὁ τὸν νόμον θεὶς τοῦτον ἦν τὸ πρῶτον
sections.translation: Was it not then a man like you and me , who first proposed this law , and by speaking persuaded the ancients ? Why then is it less lawful for me also in turn to propose henceforth a new law for the sons , that they should beat their fathers in turn ?

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: ὅσας δὲ πληγὰς εἴχομεν πρὶν τὸν νόμον τεθῆναι ,
sections.translation: But as many blows as we received before the law was made , we remit : and we concede to them our having been thrashed without return . Observe the cocks and these other animals , how they punish their fathers ; and yet , in what do they differ from us , except that they do not write decrees ?

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ , ἐπειδὴ τοὺς ἀλεκτρυόνας ἅπαντα μιμεῖ ,
sections.translation: Why then , since you imitate the cocks in all things , do you not both eat dung and sleep on a perch ?

sections.label: Section 1432
sections.original: οὐ ταὐτὸν ὦ τᾶν ἐστίν , οὐδʼ ἂν Σωκράτει δοκοίη .
sections.translation: It is not the same thing , my friend ; nor would it appear so to Socrates .

sections.label: Section 1433
sections.original: πρὸς ταῦτα μὴ τύπτʼ · εἰ δὲ μή , σαυτόν ποτʼ αἰτιάσει .
sections.translation: Therefore do not beat me ; otherwise you will one day blame yourself .

sections.label: Section 1434
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ;
sections.translation: Why , how ?

sections.label: Section 1434b
sections.original: ἐπεὶ σὲ μὲν δίκαιός εἰμʼ ἐγὼ κολάζειν ,
sections.translation: Since I am justly entitled to chastise you ; and you to chastise your son , if you should have one .

sections.label: Section 1435b
sections.original: ἢν δὲ μὴ γένηται ,
sections.translation: But if I should not have one , I shall have wept for nothing , and you will die laughing at me .

sections.label: Section 1437
sections.original: ἐμοὶ μὲν ὦνδρες ἥλικες δοκεῖ λέγειν δίκαια ·
sections.translation: To me , indeed , O comrades , he seems to speak justly ; and I think we ought to concede to them what is fitting . For it is proper that we should weep , if we do not act justly .

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: σκέψαι δὲ χἀτέραν ἔτι γνώμην .
sections.translation: Consider still another maxim .

sections.label: Section 1440b
sections.original: ἀπὸ γὰρ
sections.translation: No ; for I shall perish if I do .

sections.label: Section 1441
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἴσως γʼ οὐκ ἀχθέσει παθὼν ἃ νῦν πέπονθας .
sections.translation: And yet perhaps you will not be vexed at suffering what you now suffer .

sections.label: Section 1442
sections.original: πῶς δή ; δίδαξον γὰρ τί μʼ ἐκ τούτων ἐπωφελήσεις .
sections.translation: How , pray ? For inform me what good you will do me by this .

sections.label: Section 1443
sections.original: τὴν μητέρʼ ὥσπερ καὶ σὲ τυπτήσω .
sections.translation: I will beat my mother , just as I have you .

sections.label: Section 1443b
sections.original: τί φῄς , τί φῂς σύ ;
sections.translation: What do you say ? What do you say ? This other , again , is a greater wickedness .

sections.label: Section 1444b
sections.original: τί δʼ ἢν ἔχων τὸν ἥττω
sections.translation: But what if , having the worst Cause ,

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: λόγον σε νικήσω λέγων
sections.translation: I shall conquer you in arguing , proving that it is right to beat one ’ s mother ?

sections.label: Section 1447
sections.original: τί δʼ ἄλλο γʼ ἢν ταυτὶ ποιῇς ,
sections.translation: Most assuredly , if you do this , nothing will hinder you from casting yourself

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: μετὰ Σωκράτους
sections.translation: and your Worse Cause into the pit along with Socrates .

sections.label: Section 1452
sections.original: ταυτὶ διʼ ὑμᾶς ὦ Νεθέλαι πέπονθʼ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: These evils have I suffered through you , O Clouds ! Having intrusted all my affairs to you .

sections.label: Section 1454
sections.original: αὐτὸς μὲν οὖν σαυτῷ σὺ τούτων αἴτιος ,
sections.translation: Nay , rather , you are yourself the cause of these things , having turned yourself to wicked courses .

sections.label: Section 1456
sections.original: τί δῆτα ταῦτʼ οὔ μοι τότʼ ἠγορεύετε ,
sections.translation: Why , pray , did you not tell me this , then , but excited with hopes a rustic and aged man ?

sections.label: Section 1458
sections.original: ἡμεῖς ποιοῦμεν ταῦθʼ ἑκάστοθʼ ὅταν τινὰ
sections.translation: We always do this to him whom we perceive to be a lover of wicked courses ,

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: ἕως ἂν αὐτὸν ἐμβάλωμεν ἐς κακόν ,
sections.translation: until we precipitate him into misfortune , so that he may learn to fear the gods .

sections.label: Section 1462
sections.original: ὤμοι πονηρά γʼ ὦ Νεφέλαι , δίκαια δέ .
sections.translation: Ah me ! it is severe , O Clouds ! But it is just ; for I ought not to have withheld the money which I borrowed . Now , therefore , come with me , my dearest son ,

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: τὸν Χαιρεφῶντα τὸν μιαρὸν καὶ Σωκράτη
sections.translation: that you may destroy the blackguard Chaerephon and Socrates , who deceived you and me .

sections.label: Section 1467
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἂν ἀδικήσαιμι τοὺς διδασκάλους .
sections.translation: I will not injure my teachers .

sections.label: Section 1468
sections.original: ναὶ ναὶ καταιδέσθητι πατρῷον Δία .
sections.translation: Yes , yes , reverence Paternal Jove .

sections.label: Section 1469
sections.original: ἰδού γε Δία πατρῷον · ὡς ἀρχαῖος εἶ .
sections.translation: Paternal Jove quoth ’ a ! How antiquated you are ! Why , is there any Jove ?

sections.label: Section 1470b
sections.original: ἔστιν .
sections.translation: There is .

sections.label: Section 1470c
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ , οὔκ , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: There is not , no ; for Vortex reigns having expelled Jupiter .

sections.label: Section 1472
sections.original: οὐκ ἐξελήλακʼ , ἀλλʼ ἐγὼ τοῦτʼ ᾠόμην
sections.translation: He has not expelled him ; but I fancied this , on account of this Vortex here . Ah me , unhappy man ! When I even took you who are of earthenware for a god .

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα σαυτῷ παραφρόνει καὶ φληνάφα .
sections.translation: Here rave and babble to yourself .

sections.label: Section 1476
sections.original: οἴμοι παρανοίας · ὡς ἐμαινόμην ἄρα ,
sections.translation: Ah me , what madness ! How mad , then , I was when I ejected the gods on account of Socrates ! But O dear Hermes , by no means be wroth with me , nor destroy me ; but pardon me ,

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.original: ἐμοῦ παρανοήσαντος ἀδολεσχίᾳ ·
sections.translation: since I have gone crazy through prating . And become my adviser , whether I shall bring an action and prosecute them , or whatever you think . You advise me rightly , not permitting me to get up a lawsuit , but as soon as possible to set fire to the house

sections.label: Section 1485
sections.original: τῶν ἀδολεσχῶν . δεῦρο δεῦρʼ ὦ Ξανθία ,
sections.translation: of the prating fellows . Come hither , come hither , Xanthias ! Come forth with a ladder and with a mattock and then mount upon the thinking-shop and dig down the roof , if you love your master , until you tumble the house upon them .

sections.label: Section 1490
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δὲ δᾷδʼ ἐνεγκάτω τις ἡμμένην ,
sections.translation: But let some one bring me a lighted torch and I ’ ll make some of them this day suffer punishment , even if they be ever so much impostors .

sections.label: Section 1493
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰού .
sections.translation: ( from within ). Hollo ! Hollo !

sections.label: Section 1494
sections.original: σὸν ἔργον ὦ δᾲς ἱέναι πολλὴν φλόγα .
sections.translation: It is your business , O torch , to send forth abundant flame .

sections.label: Section 1495
sections.original: ἄνθρωπε , τί ποιεῖς ;
sections.translation: What are you doing , fellow ?

sections.label: Section 1495b
sections.original: ὅ τι ποιῶ ; τί δʼ ἄλλο γʼ ἢ
sections.translation: What am I doing ? Why , what else , than chopping logic with the beams of your house ?

sections.label: Section 1497
sections.original: οἴμοι τίς ἡμῶν πυρπολεῖ τὴν οἰκίαν ;
sections.translation: ( from within ). Ah me ! Who is setting fire to our house ?

sections.label: Section 1498
sections.original: ἐκεῖνος οὗπερ θοἰμάτιον εἰλήφατε .
sections.translation: The man whose cloak you have taken .

sections.label: Section 1499
sections.original: ἀπολεῖς ἀπολεῖς .
sections.translation: ( from within ). You will destroy us ! You will destroy us !

sections.label: Section 1499b
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὐτὸ γὰρ καὶ βούλομαι ,
sections.translation: For I also wish this very thing ;

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.original: ἢν ἡ σμινύη μοι μὴ προδῷ τὰς ἐλπίδας ,
sections.translation: unless my mattock deceive my hopes , or I should somehow fall first and break my neck .

sections.label: Section 1502
sections.original: οὗτος τί ποιεῖς ἐτεὸν οὑπὶ τοῦ τέγους ;
sections.translation: ( from within ). Hollo you ! What are you doing , pray , you fellow on the roof ?

sections.label: Section 1503
sections.original: ἀεροβατῶ καὶ περιφρονῶ τὸν ἥλιον .
sections.translation: I am walking on air , and speculating about the sun .

sections.label: Section 1504
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλας δείλαιος ἀποπνιγήσομαι .
sections.translation: Ah me , unhappy ! I shall be suffocated , wretched man !

sections.label: Section 1505
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ κακοδαίμων γε κατακαυθήσομαι .
sections.translation: And I , miserable man , shall be burnt to death !
